introduction.p: Identify a variable you might use to represent each quantity. Then identify what units would be most appropriate.

statement.p: Let be the age of a person, measured in .

solution.p: The unknown quantity is age, which we generally measure in years. So we could define this variable as:
Let a be the age of a person, measured in years.

statement.p: Let be the distance traveled by a driver that left Portland, Oregon, bound for Boise, Idaho, measured in .

solution.p: The distance traveled is the unknown quantity. Since this is a drive from Portland to Boise, it would make sense to measure this in miles. So we could define this variable as:
Let d be the distance traveled by a driver that left Portland, Oregon, bound for Boise, Idaho, measured in miles.

statement.p: Let be the surface area of the walls of a room, measured in .

solution.p: The unknown quantity is area. Apartment area is usually measured in square feet. So well define this variable as:
Let A be the area of a two-bedroom apartment, measured in \text{ft}^2\text{.}
